tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: You are given an axial abdominal CT image. What organ is shown (indicated by the arrow)?

dialogs.thought: Check for any labels or laterality markers on the CT image that might indicate the organ/side.
Orient to modality/anatomy and identify the structure indicated by the arrow.

content.content: Axial contrast-enhanced CT image of the upper abdomen. The liver is on the left side of the image, the spleen and stomach/colon gas are on the right side, and the vertebral body is central. A black arrow points to a soft-tissue structure near the upper pole of the right kidney in the right upper abdomen.

arguments.attribute: Describe the structure at the arrow location and its relationship to adjacent organs (kidney/liver/IVC).

dialogs.thought: Confirm the arrowed structure morphology and location consistent with adrenal gland.

content.content: Within the boxed region there is a small, triangular/ovoid soft-tissue structure immediately superior and medial to the upper pole of the right kidney, adjacent to the posterior aspect of the liver and near the inferior vena cava—appearance and location are consistent with the right adrenal gland.

dialogs.content: The image shows the right adrenal gland, which is a small endocrine gland located above the kidney.
